title: Akko Keycaps Set, Black&Bronze Theme 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Keycap Set with Black Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box
features: BLACK&BRONZE THEME – The combination with classic black, gray and dark bronze,　the Black&Bronze keycaps brings heavy sense of history. The bronze charactors shine on the dark keycaps let us fantasy the bronze gleams in the dark night through thick fog.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 158-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including but not limited to 61-key(60%), 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%) and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure switches including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh etc. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Some of the best keycaps under $100
reviews.text:
These keycaps are fantastic. The quality of material is noticeable right when you open the package, and the layout compatibility is uncomparable. For about $60 you get keycaps that work with the following layouts:

Full Size
1800
96%
75%
65%
60%
40% (non ortholinear)

The only layouts that aren't supported are ortholinear (though you might be able to make something work) and split (no split spacebar here). 60% with arrows (the 64-key) also is not supported as there is no 2u shift.

The colors are fantastic, the material feels very nice, and the cap profile is just incredibly comfortable to type on. In fact, it's so comfortable the only caps that I think are nicer are Drop's MT3 which cost double and don't come with as much layout variation without buying extra kits.

This specific set, Dracula's Castle, also comes with custom novelites and two different colors worth of compatibility (crimson and black) for a lot of the keys to make your board look exactly the way you want.

I'd highly recommend anything in ASA profile. The Cherry profile is good too, but not s comfortable for me to type on personally.

reviews.title: So amazing for the price
reviews.text: They looks so good for the price, Akko does it again!

reviews.title: Great quality double shot pbt keycaps.
Pretty Great Set with an Interesting Profile
reviews.text: This set is pretty nice, unlike most of Akko's keycap sets this set doesn't directly rip off color ways from existing GMK or EBPT sets, it comes close to something like SA Bliss but it adds black mods so it's not exactly the same.  The keycaps themselves are fairly nice and the font of the legends are a lot more consistent than their cherry profile sets. The packing is nice albeit a little frustrating as you pretty much need to use a keycap puller to remove the keycaps. The ASA profile is what I found the most interesting, if I had to compare it to anything I would actually compare it to OEM profile but slightly taller with rounded edges than something SA profile, The sculpting is a lot more derivative of OEM than SA in my opinion. The typing feel is pretty great it is very reminiscent of OEM profile so if you are used to OEM profile you will be right at home. The sound is more clacky than thocky at least in my NK65 with Gateron Cap Gold Switches. Overall the keycaps are nice and the ASA profile is a pleasant surprise coming from someone that uses both SA and OEM profile fairly regularly.

reviews.title: Good quality, Vivid colors, Nice sounding, Great value
reviews.text:
Akko Los Angeles 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Full Keycap Set for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box

I really love the profile of these keycaps. I have a hard time with whether to call this company a knock off company or not. They do have their own color combos and make combos that other companies do for a lot cheaper. Are color combos and fonts really proprietary? I'm not sure and eventually there will be no more combination of colors that one company will be able to claim as only their colors. I do think that Akko make really good keycaps and you get a lot for your money. So that's my two cents.

title: FINEDAY Keyboard 2.0, Red Switch, Retro Wireless Mechanical Keyboard, Typewriter Designed, Full 104 Keys with Bluetooth 5.0 & USB up to 4 Devices, Wired & Wireless for Desktop PC/Laptop Mac/Phone
features: 【 Connectivity 】 With Bluetoooth 5.0 connect up to 3 wireless devices & one USB connection, Use USB & Bluetooth at the same time total of 4 devices. FINEDAY Keyboard 2.0 "compatible with" iPad Pro size devices, Easily put the device on the holder
【 Red switch keys 】 Enjoy the Ultimate Retro Typewriter Keyboard with Smooth Feels without Clickety-Clackety sound
【 FN+TAB 】 Wired & Wireless mode to enjoy your typing in both way
【 Left lever 】 works as an Enter key just like old typewriter, Enjoy your typing experiences just like you're using an old typewriter from 1874
【 Left wheel 】 controls the LED backlight brightness. Clean and sharp white LED backlight
【 Right wheel 】 controls the volume of the connected device, No need to touch your device to adjust the sound, Easily roll the wheel!
【 Compatible 】 Windows/iOS/Android
【 Number pad 】With a number pad, with full 104 keys, easily work with numbers
【 Ergonomic 】 A 9.22-degree cradling angle for ergonomic comfort: Easily stow your devices in the mounting stand to type for long time intervals without feeling any strain on your neck

【 NEWTRO 】 A retro typewriter keyboard that lets you experience NEWTRO (New Retro) with the most up-to-date technology, Immerse yourself in nostalgia and get those creative juices flowing while boosting typing speed and accuracy. Designed & Produced by FINEDAY, Every details are designed by FINEDAY

reviews.title: Great for the look, 10 key A plus, Functional enough for device swap
reviews.text:
I really wanted this keyboard for the typewriter look, device swap, and 10-key.

The only setback is that even when plugged in via USB to the main device when waking up there is a few seconds of buffering time before being able to type at times. At first, it drove me crazy but now I notice that the green light signals when this is happening and goes solid when ready which alleviates the "let's go" anxiety because I know what's happening. I love that it pairs to 3 devices. 2 via Bluetooth and one via usb.

Another weird thing is maybe at least 1 time a week I have to unplug and plug the keyboard back in when I cannot type on my device and I know that the correct device is selected and the solid green light is on. If there were a better typewriter-style keyboard in all black and chrome with 10-key and pairs to at least 3 devices that don't have the buffering when swapping device or intermittent typing issue, I would have picked it but it doesn't exist.

Overall 4 stars.

Update:

Key caps are already falling apart after 3 months use! My heart is so heavy it’s so noticeable and I cannot find a replacement key hundreds of dollars down the drain.

reviews.title: Keys are dim
reviews.text: I sent mine back because I couldnt see the keys when I was 2 feet away. The backlight shines more between the keys than through them. It was ok in a very dark room but a dim indoor setting they were impossible to see unless you were right over the keyboard, even with the light turned all the way up. They were also kind of wobbly, cant see anyone typing fast on this. I bought a querty writer and the quality difference is huge.

reviews.title: Love the retro feel and "noise"
reviews.text: Love the retro feel and clacking noise.  Typing accuracy has improved!  The ability to switch between 3 devices is extremely convenient!

reviews.title: Not for Certain Noise
reviews.text:
Unfortunately these o-rings didn't help with the noise from my mechanical keyboard and switches. The noise from my keyboard comes from the sound the springs make when the button pops back up, and these o-rings work to muffle sound when the keys are pressed down. For keyboards with raised keys that don't bottom out, like mine, and for switches that make more noise when they come back up instead of when they go down, the o-rings are not effective for reducing noise.

Otherwise, the product is good, and I'm keeping the o-rings for when I eventually change out the switches on my keyboard entirely.

reviews.title: awesome!
reviews.text: awesome set of o-rings for the price.

reviews.title: Really nice modification for my keyboard
reviews.text: These cut down the travel and damp the clicks in my moonlander, great value.

reviews.title: Good value rings kit
reviews.text:
I was setting up two keyboards with lighter switches, and think that o-rings are important when you drop the force. This kit was a good value, and I like the brushes to clean the keyboards.

I would recommend a decent set of tweezers to push the rings down onto the key posts. I had a plastic set that was perfect for it ( they came with a pair of Moondrop IEMs ).

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Very good O-rings

Dye Sublimation：The keycaps are made of durable and anti-grease PBT material. Compared with ABS, PBT has better high hardness, wear resistance and high temperature resistance.

Wide Selection of Keycaps：Consists of 139 keycaps and other stabilizers, suitable for 90% of 61/87/104/108 mechanical and optical keyboards, as well as any key switches with cross-shaped shafts, ideal for custom mechanical keyboards select.

reviews.title: Good quality, thick keycaps
reviews.text: Fits my case perfectly. Feels nice and looks nice.

reviews.title: Good quality and adorable!
reviews.text:
Key caps are textured, but do not feel rough. The textured PBT will keep them from getting shiny as fast (compared to smooth ABS). No print errors. Fit the Cherry switches perfectly. They feel weighty and durable. The plastic is cut cleanly, no rough edges. Nice big font, centered on each key. I am very happy with these.

The only thing I am a bit disappointed about is that the special (non-standard) Capslock key does not fit my keyboard; the slot where you are supposed to insert the switch is not centered correctly. But, it isn't enough of a disappointment for me to knock down my rating.

reviews.title: Perfect!
reviews.text: These keycaps are made of PBT, which is about the highest grade of plastic that you can get for your keycaps. I love the flatter profile of the XDA keycaps. The print on the keys is very classic, large, and easy to read. This keycap set contains both peach and sea green keycaps for the function keys, space bar, escape, and enter keys. You can mix and match them until your hearts delight. You even get some extra keys for media keys if your keyboard has them, plus menu and function keys. You get a wide variety of keys that will fit most Cherry MX style keyboards easily. The plastic is nice and textured so your fingers won't slip off the keycaps when you are typing on the keyboard. I would highly recommend these keycaps as an upgrade to ABS keycaps, and for customizing your keyboard in a cute vintage style.

reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text: The middle post is made of solid metal, which isnt bad... but depending on the location of the LED under the key, the solid post can hinder the light from being spread evenly as seen in the picture I uploaded. Kinda wish the middle post was clear for better light distribution. Other than that its great. Mostly solid metal, so its quite heavy.

reviews.title: So cute
reviews.text: My husband bought this by accident when building my computer. Before it arrived I was going to return it but then it came. I love it. The pad part is squishy and it fits my keyboard. So cute!

reviews.title: Works great.

title: SteelSeries PRISMCAPS - Double Shot Pudding-Style Keycaps - Durable PBT Thermoplastic - Compatible with a Wide Range of Mechanical Keyboards - White
description: Built for extreme durability with thermoplastic PBT keycaps and with enhanced RGB pudding-style lighting, PrismCaps include 2 additional sets of bottom row keycaps, making them compatible with the widest range of MX stem mechanical keyboards.
features: Ultra-wide compatibility with both standard and non-standard bottom row keycap sets to fit most mechanical keyboard brands, including SteelSeries, Razer, Corsair, and virtually all other MX stem mechanical keyboards
Standard US ANSI 104 keys works on full sized, tenkeyless, and 60% mini keyboard layouts
Enhanced lighting displays brilliant pudding-style RGB illumination through a specially engineered double shot design with translucent sides and letters
Retains consistent texture and appearance even after heavy use with highly durable double shot PBT keycaps
Included keycap puller is built with finger hole grip to pull any keycap out with ease and flexible enough to fit around any standard keycap.

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

title: DROP + Invyr Holy Panda Mechanical Keyboard Switches - Plate Mounted Tactile Switches, Cherry MX Style, 67g Spring, by Kailh (70 PCS)
features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

title: YMDK ANSI ISO PBT Doubleshot Shine Through Pudding Keycaps Suitable for MX Mechanical Keyboard Corsair Razer SF KBD75 Tada68 DZ60 (Black Pudding Full Set)(Only Keycap)

reviews.title: Looks great, shows off my RGB light on my Keychron  K4 now.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:2f9f1dbaca0dc20e2ddfad733f0e051c]] Looks great, shows off my RGB lights on my Keychron K4 now. The only downside is not having the function icons on the keys anymore. I’ll have to work on getting some little stickers.
Edit:Added a Movie Clip

reviews.title: Great but some keys may not be suitable for your keyboard
reviews.text: they look really pretty tbh. However some keys had fitment issues. The spacebar had their slots just a little bit off so it couldnt fit. The caps lock is upside down. The windows and menu keys did not like being next to other keys. Other than that, the rest of the keys fit great.

reviews.title: They work great
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:eaa9e5229cc02ed31da7ef98d0a78ee4]] Replaced the most of my bottom row on my keyboard with these key caps.  They are good, the LED shines through and it really nice! for Corsair keyboards.

reviews.title: Quality can be improved but overall great price and compatibility
reviews.text: One of the few sets that actually fit my K95 (Corsair bottom row) Plenty of extra pieces for other keyboards too. The color black of these pieces appear much more grey compared to he caps my keyboard came with. And each piece has a little dimple at the top where they were cast or molded. Not the end of the world, but other sets don't have that. Also found small imperfections in several pieces. Time will tell how they do over prolonged use. Pretty good for the price, solid 4 of 5.

reviews.title: Just what I needed
reviews.text: I wanted some keycaps to match the theme of my keyboard but it's a struggle finding keycaps that fit the sizing of R1 on a Corsair keyboard. This is exactly what I needed!

reviews.title: So far so good! Note to buyers with costar stabilizers
reviews.text: Nice quality for the price, easy to install, keys came in a plastic tray so you can store your old keycaps in order if needed. Note to those with costar stabilizers, the key caps do not come with MX Keycap Stabilizer Inserts... so if your old keys are like mine and the old ones are apparently glued in, you'll need these to finish the keyboard. You can find them to 3d print pretty easily.

reviews.title: Printed partially
reviews.text: The letters are not oriented fully. Bothers me because I can lines through the letters.

reviews.title: I type so well on these
reviews.text: I didn't think a different key profile would do much for me, but my accuracy immediately went up with the XDA profile. These were a great price among the similar sets I found and were an affordable way to dive into my first customization. I wanted some kinda retro keys and I just love em!

title: Akko Pink Keycaps Set, Clear Translucent 155-Key ASA Profile Keys with Mac Keycaps Set for Mechanical Keyboard
features: TRANSPARENT KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Clear keycap sets! Made by polycarbonate, the keycap is solid and translucent so that your switches and RGB array can shine through. Characters on the ASA Clear keycaps are processed through UV printing with wear-resistant and scratch-resistant tests, and are not easily wiped off.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 155-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

MAC KEYCAPS & NOVELTY KEYS – The keycap set is designed for both Windows and Mac users, where Mac modifiers CMD/OPT are offered in different sizes. The transparent keycap set also features extra cute animal themed novelty keys for decoration.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: Impressed
reviews.text: Very good packaging and acceptable quality

reviews.title: Unique keys look cool with RGB. Some drawbacks.
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Beautifully and conveniently packaged and organized. Caps come on three trays with plastic film on them so that nothing will have shifted in transit. It's very easy to replace your caps and keep your old set save and organized in the trays, too. Simple to find any specialized keys that you may need in the trays.
- Print is easy to see and seems durable/high contrast. No funky fonts that are hard to read.
- Looks awesome with RGB.

Cons:

- There are no tactile nubs on the F and J keys to indicate homerow. As a touch typist, this was almost a dealbreaker.
- Color is way off from the images in the description. The images in the description show a clear white color to the keycaps. Along with the "clear/translucent" descriptors, I believed I was going to get more of a white/iced translucent look. Nope. The caps are basically colorless clear. They are smooth and glossy with no texture. I left some of the OG white keys on my keyboard to show the color clearly side by side. Not the look I was expecting from the images and description.

Good to know:

- I've never used ASA profile before, and generally prefer XDA, so these are a little taller than I'm used to on XDA, but more rounded (and, I think, more comfortable, than OEM. I had some lousy stock OEM caps on my old keyboard, and I do feel these caps are an improvement. They are sufficiently dense, and give me a slightly deeper, more muted sound from my keypresses with my brown switches, which I like, compared to the more plasticky/hollow caps that were original to my board.
- Despite having a glossy finish, I don't find these keys to be slippery--the slight curvature leads your fingertips toward the center of the keys easily and they're comfortable to use.
- I really hate that there are no homerow nubs. Who skips that? Come on.

reviews.title: No tactile bumps on F and J, amazing for backlighting
reviews.text:
I have the “White” color option in this set of keycaps from Akko. White is a bit misleading, the color is actually glossy and nearly translucent. This is actually what I wanted, I was looking all over for nearly clear keycaps, but I did want to point that out.

If you like maximum backlighting, this might be the set for you. I was using white pudding caps before, and I thought they let a lot of light shine through. The difference is night and day. These Akko caps show backlighting on all sides and the top of the cap. I’ve included a picture of a white pudding cap surrounded by the clear Akkos to show the difference.

I’m using the keycaps with Akko Vintage White (MX) switches. The keycaps fit well, but they took a bit of force to fit on the switches.

I am disappointed the F and J keys do not have ridges or bumps. I’m a touch typist and using an ergo board with 56 keys, I rely on those ridges to help me orient my hands. This set has so much variety that I wish Akko had included doubles of F and J or even blanks with the ridges.

Speaking of keycap variety: the set has some great 1u options! My current keyboard is an Iris Rev 6, all keys are 1u size. But most keycap sets don’t have 1u keycaps for Shift, Control, Command/Windows, and so on. This set does, which is a big help. I also like the cute animal keycaps, they add some fun to my board.

I believe the ASA height is original to Akko. They have a helpful height chart on their site, comparing ASA to OEM, JDA, OSA, and Cherry. I was using OEM before, and switching to ASA and the glossy feel of the keycaps took about two days of adjustment.

title: GTSP 9009 XDA Retro PBT Keycaps Set for Gaming Keyboard Dye-Sub for Cherry Mx Gateron Kailh Switch GK61/GK68 60% Mechanical Keyboard (9009-XDA)

✅ 【Switch Compatibility】-Standardized MX compatible with most MX switches. e.g. Cherry MX switches and clones including Gateron, Halo, and Kaihua

reviews.title: Space bar was too big:( still cute tho

reviews.title: Really cheap look and feel
reviews.text:
These are really cheap. I know you might be thinking, you get what you pay for and these are cheaply priced. However, at the end of the day, keycaps are just plastic and the good ones are just waaay over priced.

First thing first, the actual color differs from the picture. The picture has a nice looking matcha green, the actual product is a little darker green and no where as vibrant. Also, some of the legends on the keycaps are kind of blurry. I am noticing the blurriness on all the non-alpha caps. You can tell these are really cheap and they look pretty bad.

reviews.title: doesnt work correctly for gmmk tkl
reviews.text: bought these to replace the stabilizers in my gmmk tk; board ... for some reason they just dont work.  they dont correctly click in and if you put a keycap on it and take the keycap off it pulls the whole thing with it. ended up just using the gold bars and white switch part with the other stuff from my gmmk.  its better than stock at least.

reviews.title: Everglides and they are nice

reviews.title: Cure clicky stabs
reviews.text:
Since you always gotta replace PCB manufacturer stabs, you might as well buy these. Great quality. Highly recommend full Holee mod for the best feel. Always lube your stabs folks.
highly recommend.

reviews.title: Improvement on V1
reviews.text: I have used the first version of these and they work good, but have had issues with the stabilizer wires popping out when removing keycaps.  These updated V2 have extra hook to keep the wire in place and work nicely.  These are some of the best stabilizers you can get right now.

reviews.title: I should have gotten these a while ago...
reviews.text:
I have an NK87 and the stabilizer ticking was terrible. I did all the tricks for it, but it persisted from some keys. NK sells their stabilizers for about the same price as these, so I thought I would just have to live with it. Then I just decided to get these, and am so glad I did. Ticking is gone.

Key points
1. They look extremely similar to the NKs, but there is obviously some difference.
2. Straight and flat wires - no tuning needed
3. Manufactured right - i.e. no need to clip, stems are flat
4. Came with the soulmate tuning kit. I think this helped a lot too.

Highly recommended.

Easy to charge and can charge it while using it.

They returned my email quickly and was eager to resolve my problem, which they did.  I am extremely pleased with the keyboard and customer support I received.

features: ♫♫♫AJAZZ Diced Fruit blueberry switch for mechanical keyboard top cap and button part of the housing are made of transparent light blue and dark blue, blue-gray POM shaft core adds dust-proof wall, which increases the stability when pressing, and provides you with a smoother typing experience.

♫♫♫5 pin keyboard blueberry switches is the latest light pressure linear switch are made with operating force of 35gf and total travel of 3.8mm, 15.5mm double spring is more smooth when typing, it is perfectly compatible with 5pin hotswap mechanical keyboard.

♫♫♫AJAZZ fruit series custom switches in order to satisfy you who like DIY, we introduce different tactile or linear switches, whether beginners, professional gamers, typists and programmers can choose the most suitable AJAZZ fruit series switches. Create a unique typing experience for you.

reviews.title: Light, snappy, like a punchier Gat brown
reviews.text:
I previously got a tactile switch sampler set and honestly loved the AJAZZ Banana more than U4 Boba and its other iterations. I liked these more than Glorious Panda and Holy Panda. I type a lot as a student, so I didn't want anything too heavy--it gets tiring. I've been using AJAZZ Banana for two days so far now and I LOVE it!

I was using lubed Gateron browns before. I wanted something punchier but not too heavy. Banana is the perfect answer to that. Actuation force is about the same to Gat browns. I have a north-facing RGB board and the lights come through Banana switches beautifully.

In answer to the other reviewer, I believe that the Banana switches ARE lubed (with what, I don't know). I have a stock/un-lubed AJAZZ Banana switch from my switch sampler pack, these pre-lubed ones feel slightly lighter in comparison. Out of the box, these feel great without additional lubing. Results will vary depending on your mech keeb setup.

Note that the switch pack comes with 46 switches, so it won't be enough to do a full board. I have a 60% board and had enough to do the alphanumeric keys and other frequently pressed keys like Shift, Enter, Ctrl, Backspace, Alt, Tab, and punctuation keys. I left my Gat browns on the less frequent keys. I have two "extra" Banana switches as backups.

Highly recommend. If you want tactile but not heavy, this is a very affordable option.

reviews.title: Bananas are excellent tactile switch alternatives to browns and blues.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:dff950435419a7006b0295e50b846c44]] This review is for the Ajazz Diced Fruit Banana Switch.
I wanted to try these switches compared to a brown switch because of its similar characteristics.
These Banana switches are definitely tactile and smooth. They do have a tactile click sound and require a bit more force to actuate (not really noticeable for me because I'm heavy handed and I bottom out the key when I type). I'm not going to mention specs such as actuation force and distance because you can find that info in the product description or manufacturer's site.
I will talk about the "feeling" of these switches though because in everyday use, gaming and office work, I'm not thinking about the actuation force/distance, etc. For me it is how it feels and sounds.

So first off, these are excellent switches, no doubt. It is then just a matter of personal preference whether or not you like the characteristics of the Banana switch. For me, it is a bit too noisy. The tactile bump feels good but after using the Banana's for a while, I would hate typing while on mic because the mic picks up the keystrokes loud and clear and can be annoying for others to hear. I don't have blue switches to compare these Bananas to but I would imagine the blue switches would be worse in sound. I do have red switches to compare and the reds are noticeably quieter, I think mainly due to being linear (no tactile bump). For me and personal preference and usage, I would stick with the reds.

If you like tactile switches and are currently using a different tactile switch, I'd urge you give these Banana switches a try. See my video review where I installed these switches into a keyboard and compare to red switches, so you can get an idea of the sound when the switches are installed into a keyboard frame with keycaps on. In the video I have mistakenly called the red switches Gaterons, but I'm pretty sure they are Kailh reds with box stem.

reviews.title: Nice looking strong tactile
reviews.text:
I love tactile switches, so when this was offered for review I figured I'd give it a shot as I'd heard good things about Ajazz switches. It arrived and I swapped out the switches in my NK65 (DROP Holy Pandas) and threw these into the alphas.

In terms of feel they're a pretty strong tactile. I felt it important to mention that I was using HP's above because these feel very similar to those, just with a lighter spring. The bump is close to identical which is nice, and I prefer the lighter spring as I've been mainlining my Niz lately with 35g switches, so light is my thing right now. In terms of sound, the housing does make these quite a bit higher pitch than HP's, and I'd honestly put it in line with some Akko tactiles. Not too high to not enjoy, but higher than I'm initially used to.

Because these come pre-lubed they can be used stock, which is nice because while once I start I find lubing switches to be relaxing, but it's an activity I dread until I'm actually going. Just something about it seems beyond tedious, and to be fair, it is. Thankfully the lube job on these is pretty good and there's no ping to be heard or scratchiness to be felt. I did lube one just to see the difference that one done by hand can make, and the job on these is good enough that it makes no difference. The switch is fine as-is.

Other than being plate mount only I can't find a problem with these. The spring weight is nice and they sound and feel great to type on. I'm thinking about grabbing another set to build out a board with them in the future.

reviews.title: Kiwi switches
reviews.text:
This is a review for the Kiwi Switch from DAIDAI AJAZZ. The Kiwi switch is tactile, 50gf, and housed in glittery translucent green. If you have clear keycaps, you can see the yellow stem and a little of the upper housing.

For the Kiwi switches, you get 46 switches. For most keyboards, 46 switches is not enough and you will need 2-3 cans.

The switches come in a clear plastic can, with a screw lid and a pull tab. The packaging is cute! More importantly, the can takes up less space than the usual plastic tray, and it’s reusable.

I use Akko Vintage Whites (linear), but I wanted to try tactile switches for the thumb clusters on my ergo keyboard, so I had feedback when I pressed those keys. The tactile bump is smooth and pronounced, I definitely know I pressed that key.

I didn’t notice any difference between these pre-lubed Kiwi switches and non-lubed switches.

reviews.title: Pretty good tactile switches
reviews.text: I've tried linears and even clicky switches, but I really prefer tacticles. These are right in the sweet spot for me in terms of spring weight and tactile bump. Nice colors, if that matters to you. I got these to try out before buying a full set, and I like them quite a bit.

title: G.SKILL USB KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard, Cherry MX Red, ABS Dual Injection Keycap, (Black)
description: The G.SKILL KM360 tenkeyless mechanical keyboard is a compact 87-key layout keyboard designed for durability. Featuring long-lasting Cherry MX mechanical keyswitches, wear-proof ABS double injection keycaps, and a solid aluminum top-plate, the KM360 mechanical keyboard is the perfect daily mechanical keyboard. With a detachable USB Type-C cable, it's easy to replace a worn cable by simply swapping it out with another USB Type-C cable of your choice.
features: CHERRY MX - Most popular mechanical keyswitch for their high standards of quality, reliability, and durability; rated for over 50 million keypresses.
COMPACT & PORTABLE - Designed with a detachable USB Type-C cable and a tenkeyless layout for more desk space and great portability.
WEAR-PROOF KEYS - Using ABS dual-injection keycaps mean the keys won't wear out like printed or laser etched keycaps.
HIGH DURABILITY - Constructed with a solid aluminum top plate and floating keyswitches for easy cleaning.
MODERN DESIGN - Featuring ergonomic keys, white LED backlight, and n-key rollover for comfort and ease of use.

reviews.title: Clicky clack who’s at the rack with the jackets? Me!
reviews.text:
I had been eyeing a mechanical keyboard for years, hesitant to buy one because A) I already had a perfectly fine working keyboard and B) Reviews I had read made it sound like they were so noisy you needed to wear ear protection while using one. During a recent move I left my previously mentioned perfectly fine working keyboard outside for an extended period of time, uncovered in the rain and sun and any other elements that rolled though Southern California for those few months. When I finally returned to the keyboard, I found that it had become yet another casualty of the pandemic. Finally I had good cause for a new keyboard, and my research led me to try this one. And like the famous tattoo says, I have no ragrets!

I plugged it in and immediately it lit up and off my little fingies went, zipping around the keyboard with the grace and elegance of a gold medal winning gymnast at the 2021 Tokyo Olympics. Every click and clack was like music to my ears, each sentence I typed sounding like a symphony composed by Mozart himself! I now look forward to typing my weekly emails to mother, catching her up with her favorite son’s (sorry Richard, but you know it’s true!) comings and goings!
I’ve only had this keyboard for about a week, so time will tell if it will hold up to the rigorous poundings that accompany each email I compose, but as of now I would not hesitate to recommend this keyboard to all my many (ok, zero) friends! Now I can’t wait to get off from my day job as a clerk typist and go home and get some typing in!

reviews.title: Great Initial Impression
reviews.text:
My first foray into mechanical KB and cherry switches. I was familiar with GSkill as they are a trusted brand in performance PC DDR4 RAM. I liked the price point and the size was perfect. Smaller than a full size but still having arrow keys and delete, etc off to the side.
I’m no KB expert, but it appears to be of good quality and finish. Nice adjustable LED backlighting. It has a sleeved nylon cable that’s white and matches the board nicely. Typing on it will take some getting used to, but I’m liking it so far and will update later if there’s any issues.

reviews.title: Amazing keyboard EDIT:
reviews.text: Amazing keyboard.  I'm typing on my new G.SKILL KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard and YES it amazing!  Seriously if you have the money by this product.  Totally worth every penny! It's been just over a year since I got this mechanical keyboard & the USB type C is coming loose, I have to move the cord back N forth so it will connect again. Not sure if it can be fixed or not. (EDIT) I got this in October of 2019 & the small side of the USB Type-C female side is breaking off, causing the keyboard to flicker on and off. Other than that it's awesome keyboard

reviews.title: Great keyboard from a regular joe
reviews.text:
This is my first keyboard and saw great review. I’m not a big gamer or anything but recently started playing a little on a gaming laptop and thought why not. I thought the keyboard on the laptop were fine but damn these are better. I started using them for work and everything and love this key board now. So if your on the fence just do it.

10/10

reviews.title: Almost perfect
reviews.text: Exactly what I was looking for in an TKL board. The only thing that’s below standard are the kick stands. They don’t provide much resistance, so when you just nudge the keyboard forward the kickstands collapse - not sure if they need to be more sturdy or have a more grippy rubber foot but a rather disappointing feature to an otherwise perfect set up.

title: Happy Balls 60% Keycaps Side Printed PBT Keycap Set 60 Percent OEM Profile Thick Cherry MX Key Caps with Key Puller for US-ANSI Layout 61 68 60% MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard(Black Gray Combo)
description:
Note:
This is only keycaps, no keyboard included. This 60% keycaps set fit most standard US Layout 61 68 60% mx switches mechanical keyboards. But different brands maybe have different layout, so some keys may have different sizes. If you are not sure about the keycaps size of your keyboard, you can confirm with us.
Description:
→ The main material of this keycap set is PBT;   → The height is Semi profile,the thickness is about 1.5mm;   → This 60% keyset fit most Standard US layout 61 68 60% mx switches mechanical keyboards.
61 Keycaps Size and Height(First layer):
→ Tab: 1.5u, R3;   → Caps Lock: 1.75u, R2;   → Left Shift: 2.25u, R1;   → Ctrl,Win,Alt,Menu: 1.25u, R1;   → Spacebar: 6.25u, R1;   → Right Shift: 2.75u, R1;   → Enter: 2.25u, R2;   → Backspace: 2u, R4;   → Vertical line: 1.5u, R3;   → The size of other letters are 1u, the height of the letter is the same as the first keycap in the row of the letter .
16 Keycaps Size and Height(Second layer):
→ Right Shift: 1.75u, R1;   → Ctrl,Fn,Alt: 1u, R1;   → PgDn: 1u, R1;   → PgUp: 1u, R2;   → Del: 1u, R3;   → ~: 1u, R4;
If some keycaps are broken or missing when you receive the keyset, you can tell us , we will send these keycaps to you in time.
features: This 60% keycap set contain 77pcs pbt keycaps(non-backlit) and 1pcs keycap puller
It can fit most Standard US-ANSI layout 61 68 60% mx switches mechanical keyboard
Side printed keycaps, the patterns use laser engraving process
The height is OEM profile,the thickness is about 1.5mm

Note: There are many types of 60% keyboards. Different brands maybe have different layout, so some keys may have different sizes. If you are not sure about the keycaps size of your keyboard, you can confirm with us.

reviews.title: Nice PBT Keycaps!
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:a15d535808b6422b280045d17e175b1e]] I love the colors they choose, and the ergonomic is on point where I like it. I would definitely buy more keycaps from this seller!

title: ASCENY Coiled & Double-Sleeved Mechanical Keyboard Cable, for Type-C Mechanical Keyboards (Purple)
description: As MK enthusiasts who are obsessed with building keyboards, we understood the needs of the mechanical keyboards community. This cable is our latest take on designed mechanical keyboard cables. Our goal was to create a mass production product that will allow more people to get into the hobby without having to spend a huge amount of money. After many failed attempts to make a great coil in mass production, we finally believe that we have created a great product that stands in one with the standards of mechanical keyboard enthusiasts.
features: Stiff plastic injected Asceny connectors. No more amateurish heatshrinks.
PET double sleeve that gives a beautiful texture, and extra durability.
4.9ft long (excluding the coil length) & Super flexible 6" tight coil that lasts.
Comes in a beautiful package that holds the coil tight. Can be saved and re-used whenever you want to store your cable.

reviews.title: Great cable for a small investment.
reviews.text:
Being new to to the Mechanical Keyboard market, I recently purchased a prebuilt "budget" board that came with a long plain usb-c cable, which would be fine but I wanted a cleaner look for my desk. Aviator cables are great but I did not want to invest $50+ for a single use cable. In comes Asceny with a cable that costs half as much and looks just as good in my opinion. The packaging was nicely made, and does not feel cheap in any way, this company really put a lot of love into the product.

My only complaint is that with the direction of the coil, I can not have the Asceny logo facing up with a left side charge port on my keyboard, otherwise the cable does not sit correctly on the desk. This would not dissuade me from recommending it though, and may even encourage some buyers.

reviews.title: Keychron K4 Compatible
reviews.text:
Read reviews about the plug not fitting on some Keychrons but was prepared to modify. Turns out, it fits just fine, and I haven’t had any issues so far.
Cable itself feels solid and durable, a bit stiff, but maneuverable.
Overall, for the price, I’d say go with it if you are debating.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Good value, sturdy, nice style

reviews.title: Good starter coil cable for keyboard build
reviews.text: This is my first coil keyboard cable for my first mechanical keyboard build. I can’t compare it to one of the more popular brands like Glorious for example, but for what it is, it’s a nice, sturdy, well made cable. It looks good and is a nice accessory for my keyboard. The coils are actually smaller than I thought they would be, but still nice. I wish the cable was longer though, that’s my only complaint.

reviews.title: good wire to save space and for the *aesthetic*
reviews.text: i love how the coiled-wire pairs with the mechanical keyboard. it makes my desk look less messy and looks fab LOL

title: NPKC Black Gray Mixed Dolch Thick PBT 104 87 61 Keycaps OEM Profile Key caps for MX Mechanical Keyboard (Only Keycap) (87 Side Print)
description: This link is only the keycaps,no keyboard included.The keycap loading board is only to take photo,not included in the sold package.We will pack the keycaps in our own carton. This blank keycap set is no lengend.The printed ones are laser engraved.You can customize your own font and only need to add usd5 for your own font.Nearly all font is ok if can be opened on AI software. The keycap is made of durable PBT material,the thickness is about 1.5mm. Compatiability It can be used for most Cherry MX Gateron MX Kailh MX and all other MX switches keyboard. Incompatiability If the spacebar is 6u 6.5u 7u instead of 6.25u,it can't be compatible,such as some HHKB Cosair .You can order additional keys needed.We will produce special full set for them soon. Some old Cherry keyboard whose Spacebar Capslock back hole is different is not compatible.We have cherry 117 121 keyset owns both special Space and Capslock in other link. Some Costar stabilizer keyboard whose spacebar may be clicky for thick caps.You can adjust the wire or cut the edges of inner spacebar.This is a easy problem.If you have met this problem,just leave us message.We will help you solve it. After Service If some keys missing or broken on the way,just leave us message,we will send you missing or broken ones soon.Don't worry about it and don't leave feedback immediately.
features: This link only sells OEM profile Keycaps,no keyboard included.There is a puller presented in package.
This keycap is made of 90% PBT.The thickness is around 1.5mm.The printed one is laser etched.The blank one is no legend
This keycap is compatible with most standard ANSI MX switches keyboard whose bottom modifier key is 1x6.25u 7x1.25u.Space is around 11.7cm.
This YMDK keycap is not compatible with Corsair Razer Keyboard..64 68 75 84 96 keyboard can't use it as well
